mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate above 6.0 percent signals severe labor market distress and reduced consumer spending capacity, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 55 percent of monthly observations when the unemployment rate crosses the 6.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates robust consumer demand and credit availability, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in retail sales and housing starts fails to produce positive 60-day returns in more than 50 percent of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below negative 0.5 percent signals acute supply-side contraction and factory slowdowns, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in more than 60 percent of instances following the industrial production decline trigger.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates active monetary tightening, compressing equity valuation multiples and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55 percent of months where inflation rises above 0.3 percent while policy rates do not fall.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects broad economic expansion and rising corporate earnings expectations, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 2-year rolling window, or payroll growth shows no statistical correlation with 60-day equity direction.

mechanism_narrative: A quarterly real GDP contraction below negative 1.0 percent signals a severe aggregate demand shock and recessionary onset, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the following 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Positive 20-day forward returns occur in more than 50 percent of quarters meeting the GDP contraction trigger condition.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where industrial production falls while total employment rises indicates labor hoarding during supply bottlenecks, increasing corporate cost pressures and causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 65 percent of divergence instances, invalidating the cost-pressure transmission channel.
